description: BCOS compliance framework mapping: Semgrep rule categories and OSV severities to OWASP Top 10 2021, CWE Top 25 2023, and NIST AI RMF

A01:2021-Broken Access Control.description: Restrictions on authenticated users not properly enforced

A02:2021-Cryptographic Failures.description: Failures related to cryptography leading to sensitive data exposure

A03:2021-Injection.description: User-supplied data not validated, filtered, or sanitized

A04:2021-Insecure Design.description: Missing or ineffective control design; distinct from implementation bugs

A05:2021-Security Misconfiguration.description: Missing hardening, default configs, open cloud storage, verbose errors

A07:2021-Identification and Authenticati.description: Confirmation of identity, authentication, and session management failures

A08:2021-Software and Data Integrity Fai.description: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations

A09:2021-Security Logging and Monitoring.description: Insufficient logging, detection, monitoring, and active response

A10:2021-Server-Side Request Forgery.description: Fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL

CWE-434.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type

CWE-119.name: Improper Restriction of Operations within Memory Buffer

CWE-362.name: Concurrent Execution Using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization

CWE-94.name: Improper Control of Generation of Code (Code Injection)

GOVERN.description: Governance structures for AI risk management

categories.GV-1: Policies for responsible AI design, development, deployment
categories.GV-4: Organizational practices aligned with AI principles
categories.GV-5: Ongoing monitoring processes for deployed AI

MAP.description: Mapping context and risks of AI systems

categories.MAP-1: Intended purposes, context of use, and known limitations documented
categories.MAP-2: AI system classified for risk and impact
categories.MAP-3: AI benefits and costs assessed across stakeholders
categories.MAP-5: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities assessed

categories.MS-2: AI systems evaluated for trustworthy characteristics
categories.MS-3: Mechanisms for tracking identified risks over time
categories.MS-4: Feedback from deployment incorporated into measurements

MANAGE.description: Managing AI risks to acceptable levels

categories.MG-2: Risk treatment strategies selected and implemented
categories.MG-3: Risk management of third-party AI resources

security.injection.description: Generic injection flaws: SQL, OS command, XSS, code injection

security.audit.exec.description: Dangerous exec/system/popen calls with user input

security.audit.subprocess.description: Subprocess calls with shell=True or unsanitized arguments

security.sqli.description: SQL injection via string formatting or concatenation

security.ssrf.description: Server-side request forgery via user-controlled URLs

security.deserialization.description: Insecure deserialization of untrusted data (pickle, yaml.load, etc.)

security.crypto.description: Weak or broken cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4)

security.crypto.weak-hash.description: Use of weak hash functions for password storage or integrity

security.crypto.weak-random.description: Non-cryptographic PRNG used for security-sensitive operations

security.crypto.hardcoded-secret.description: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or secrets in source code

security.crypto.insecure-tls.description: Disabled TLS verification, insecure protocol versions, weak cipher suites

security.auth.description: Authentication bypass, missing authentication, weak password policies

security.auth.jwt.description: JWT algorithm confusion, missing verification, none algorithm

security.auth.session.description: Session fixation, missing session expiry, insecure session handling

security.access-control.description: Missing or improper authorization checks, privilege escalation

security.csrf.description: Missing CSRF protection on state-changing endpoints

security.path-traversal.description: Directory traversal via user-controlled file paths

security.cors.description: Overly permissive CORS configuration (wildcard origins, credentials)

security.audit.dangerous-call.description: Use of eval(), exec(), Function() with dynamic input

security.audit.file-upload.description: Unrestricted file upload without type/size validation

security.information-disclosure.description: Sensitive data in error messages, logs, or debug output

security.header-injection.description: HTTP header injection via CRLF or unvalidated header values

security.template-injection.description: Server-side template injection (SSTI) via user-controlled templates

security.regex-dos.description: Regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) via catastrophic backtracking

security.race-condition.description: TOCTOU and other race conditions leading to security bypass

security.memory.description: Memory safety issues: buffer overflow, use-after-free, null dereference

security.integer-overflow.description: Integer overflow or underflow leading to unexpected behavior

security.logging.description: Insufficient logging, log injection, or missing audit trails

security.secrets.description: Secrets, tokens, passwords exposed in source code or config

security.misconfiguration.debug.description: Debug mode enabled in production (Flask DEBUG, Django DEBUG, etc.)

CRITICAL.description: Critical severity CVE in a dependency; immediate remediation required

HIGH.description: High severity CVE in a dependency; remediate within days

MEDIUM.description: Medium severity CVE in a dependency; remediate within weeks

LOW.description: Low severity CVE in a dependency; track and remediate at next update cycle
